example.name: simple_display_widget
example.description: Create or refine a lightweight display-oriented widget
example.context: Use this pattern for widgets similar in scope to DateOutput

steps.step: Review the existing widget structure and preview files
Implement semantic layout and scoped styles
Keep metadata and documentation proportional to the widget complexity

example.name: list_based_widget
example.description: Create or refine a widget that renders repeated items from bound data
example.context: Use this pattern for widgets similar to MultiSelect or Breadcrumb

steps.step: Inspect how list data is represented in the widget and preview examples
Document expected item shape and any relevant config options
Keep rendering logic aligned with the actual data model

example.name: advanced_widget_with_metadata
example.description: Create or refine a widget with config metadata, business objects, and events
example.context: Use this pattern for widgets similar to Stepper

steps.step: Review config metadata and business object definitions first
Update event behavior, widget implementation, and documentation together
Ensure preview assets demonstrate the advanced widget behavior clearly

example.name: handoff_to_packaging
example.description: Stop at the authoring boundary when the user requests TWX generation
example.context: Use when the widget work is complete and the user asks for packaging or deployment

steps.step: Confirm the widget implementation is complete
Do not run packaging tasks in this mode
Direct the workflow to the package manager mode
